fields.value: Enterprises need greater visibility into what their agents are doing and the ability to reverse course when an agent acts in error

value: Ability to reverse unintended agent activity in defined scenarios
Scoping the potential impact of an agent-initiated change
Identifying when an agent operates outside approved boundaries
Maintaining an auditable record of what each agent did and why
Controls aligned to NIST AI Risk Management Framework and ISO/IEC 42001
Enterprises can move agents into production without giving up oversight
Every agent action is recorded and governed
Unintended actions can be reversed in defined scenarios

evidence.quote: Initial joint work focuses on regulated sectors, including healthcare, life sciences, financial services and insurance, where auditability and operational control are central requirements.

Enterprises everywhere are working to operationalize AI, and many reach the same point of friction: they need greater visibility into what their agents are doing, and they need the ability to reverse course when an agent acts in error

By embedding Rubrik Agent Cloud into our Neuro ® AI platform and AI Factory , we intend to give clients the confidence to let agents act, because agent actions can be seen, governed and, if necessary, reversed.
The aim is to give enterprises visibility into agent actions, real-time policy enforcement, and the ability to reverse unintended agent activity in defined scenarios

Together, the two layers are intended to let enterprises move agents into production without giving up oversight. Agents act, every action is recorded and governed, and unintended actions can be reversed in defined scenarios.

fields.value: Engineers wrote test scripts by hand, ran them, read the results, and repeated the cycle many times over.

Claude Code reads chip pinouts and hardware schematics directly, then writes and runs regression tests, and compares live data from real equipment against its digital twin to flag firmware regressions and signal-integrity faults.
Engineers, architects, consultants, industry specialists, and forward-deployed engineers who sit alongside client teams.
Catch design flaws earlier, speed up chip validation, and bring hardware and software together in a single system.

value: iDEC's closed-loop pipeline already cuts validation cycle times by 50 to 70%, condensing standard four-day turnarounds into 48 hours.

evidence.quote: UST works alongside semiconductor, automotive, manufacturing, telecom, embedded, and IoT companies.
engineers write test scripts by hand, run them, read the results, and repeat the cycle many times over.

Claude Code reads chip pinouts and hardware schematics directly, then writes and runs regression tests—the checks that confirm a change to a design didn’t cause an unintended downstream effect—which engineers used to script by hand. Claude also compares the live data from real equipment against its digital twin—the software model of how that hardware is supposed to behave—and flags firmware regressions and signal-integrity faults.

UST is committing to train 20,000 of its associates on Claude worldwide, including engineers, architects, consultants, industry specialists, and forward-deployed engineers, who sit alongside client teams.
UST is aiming to catch design flaws earlier, speed up chip validation, and bring hardware and software together in a single system.
Human approval steps and audit controls are important forms of governance that make it possible for the systems to run in production.

iDEC’s closed-loop pipeline,reads hardware designs, generates and runs regression tests, and compares live equipment data against its digital twin to flag issues early, already cuts validation cycle times by 50 to 70%, condensing standard four-day turnarounds into 48 hours.
